page.title: Settings
page.subtitle: Operator configuration (/etc/patchpanel/config.yaml). Saves persist immediately but most fields require a process restart to take effect.
page.saved: Saved to disk. Click Restart now to apply, or restart manually later.

restart.title: Restart patchpanel?
restart.body: This exits the process. systemd (or the HA addon supervisor) restarts it within seconds.
restart.note: The browser tab will auto-reload after a few seconds.

errorPages.title: Error pages
errorPages.intro: Each per-status override below can be edited in one of two modes:<0/><1>Raw (.http, errorfile)</1> — full HTTP response served byte-for-byte, no token expansion.<2/><3>Log-format (lf-file)</3> — HAProxy expands <4>%[unique-id]</4>, <5>%[var(…)]</5>, <6>%[hdr(…)]</6> etc. at serve time; required if you want per-request data like the unique ID to appear in the page. Rendered as <7>http-error status N content-type "text/html; charset=utf-8" lf-file …</7>.<8/>The <9>Error pages sections</9> at the bottom define named <10>http-errors NAME</10> bundles that frontends and defaults blocks can reference via <11>useErrorFilesId</11>.

previewVars.summary: Preview variables ({{count}}) — replaces <0>%[token]</0> in the preview only
previewVars.examples: Examples: <0>unique-id</0>, <1>hdr(host)</1>, <2>var(txn.request_id)</2>, <3>ssl_fc_protocol</3>. HAProxy itself does not expand these in static errorfile bodies — for real expansion you need <4>http-response set-header</4> / <5>http-after-response</5> directives or Lua. This panel mocks them so you can iterate on the look of your template.

modeHelp.raw: Served byte-for-byte via <0>errorfile</0>. HAProxy does NOT expand <1>%[token]</1> tokens here — what you type is what hits the wire. Written to <2>{{outPath}}</2>.
modeHelp.lf: Served via <0>http-error … lf-file</0>. HAProxy <1>does</1> expand log-format tokens like <2>%[unique-id]</2>, <3>%[var(txn.x)]</3>, <4>%[hdr(host)]</4>, <5>%[src]</5> at request time. Written to <6>{{outPath}}</6>; the matching <7>http-error status {{codePh}}</7> directive is auto-injected on save.

modal.loadBundledTitle: Load the bundled .http template into the editor as a starting point
modal.rawPlaceholder:
HTTP/1.0 503 Service Unavailable
Cache-Control: no-cache
Content-Type: text/html

<html>...
modal.lfNote: Token expansion is <0>real</0> in this mode — the values below model what HAProxy will substitute at serve time.
modal.rawNote: Tokens are mocked for the preview only. In raw mode HAProxy serves the file byte-for-byte; tokens would render as literal text in production.

perStatus.title: Per-status template overrides
perStatus.description: The 19 canonical HTTP status codes. Each row shows the current path emitted into <0>haproxy.cfg</0> for this defaults block, whether a custom body override is set, and an Edit button that opens the bundled template plus an editor for the override body.
perStatus.noBlocks: No defaults blocks yet. Add one on the Defaults page first.

idSelect.noSections: No <0>http-errors</0> sections defined. Add one on the <1>Error pages</1> tab first.

loadedPathUnknown: Configuration loader has no known on-disk path

upload.targetAndContentRequired: targetPath and content are required strings
